description: Having the pleasant taste characteristic of sugar or honey; not bitter or sour. 'Kaelo' names the quality of sweetness—the taste that pleases, that signals ripeness, that rewards.
sound_symbolism: The word pleases. 'Ka' begins with clear presence, taste arriving. The vowel 'e' opens to pleasure, sweetness spreading. Then 'lo' flows with satisfaction, enjoyment complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'kaelo tiroa' (sweet fruit). Opposite of 'puko' (bitter). Can describe taste or metaphorical sweetness of experience.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sweet tastes signaled nutrition—ripeness and energy in fruits and honey, nature's rewards.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Sweet experiences are impermanent—enjoying without grasping, appreciating without demanding more.
